reference: Hi. From your history, this may be an inguinal hernia or a boil. If this is a hernia, it will be expansible—meaning it increases with coughing and straining—and reducible, meaning it can be manually reduced. If it is a boil, it will remain the same irrespective of coughing or pressing. The treatment for a hernia is herniotomy, and if it is a boil or similar condition, a trial with an antibiotic and anti-inflammatory medications for five days will be sufficient.
text: Hi, from your history, this may be an inguinal hernia or a boil. If this is a hernia, it will be expansible, meaning it increases with coughing and straining, and reducible, meaning it can be manually reduced. If it is a boil, it will remain the same irrespective of coughing or pressing. The treatment for a hernia is herniotomy, and if it is a boil or similar condition, a trial with an antibiotic and anti-inflammatory medications for five days will be sufficient.
